title: epidendrum fraternum
intro: Epidendrum fraternum, also known as the "Quebrada" or "Wild Orchid," is a species of orchid that is native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Epidendrum fraternum include relief from pain, fever reduction, and wound healing, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
